title: ProtocolLab gRPC over HTTP/2 Performance Contracts
purpose: Define deterministic, language-neutral gRPC over HTTP/2 success, boundary, lifecycle, and terminal-outcome semantics.
scope: The family covers exact gRPC over TLS-protected HTTP/2, including bounded diagnostic breadth. gRPC-Web and gRPC over HTTP/3 remain deferred.

requirements.title: Exact HTTP/2 binding
requirements.statement: gRPC performance scenarios MUST require the gRPC over HTTP/2 binding and reject HTTP/1.1, gRPC-Web, HTTP/3, or other fallback.

requirements.title: Canonical service and method
requirements.statement: gRPC scenarios MUST declare the language-neutral service contract identity and digest, method name, canonical POST path, RPC type, request content type, response content type, and metadata policy.

requirements.title: Deterministic gRPC messages
requirements.statement: gRPC scenarios MUST declare deterministic serialized request and response message bytes, lengths, SHA-256 rules, compression state, and request and response message counts.

requirements.title: gRPC completion validity
requirements.statement: Successful gRPC measurements MUST require HTTP status 200, expected content type and trailers, grpc-status zero, complete message counts and bytes, and zero unexpected failure, deadline-exceeded, cancellation, or timeout outcomes.

requirements.title: Distinct RPC lifecycle semantics
requirements.statement: Unary, server-streaming, client-streaming, and bidirectional-streaming operations MUST remain distinct scenario semantics when activated.

requirements.title: Channel and stream load identity
requirements.statement: gRPC load profiles MUST declare connections or channels, channel reuse or churn, concurrency, effective concurrent HTTP/2 streams, calls or message rate, duration, warmup, repetitions, and deadlines or timeouts.

requirements.title: gRPC metrics and artifacts
requirements.statement: gRPC scenarios MUST declare RPC latency percentiles, calls and messages per second, bytes per second, completed, failed, deadline-exceeded, cancelled and timed-out counts, and raw HTTP/2, RPC, validation, stdout, stderr, and normalized artifacts.

requirements.title: Canonical client streaming
requirements.statement: The client-streaming scenario MUST send exactly 100 ordered deterministic request messages, half-close the request stream, and accept exactly one response matching the final request payload.

requirements.title: Trailers-only status semantics
requirements.statement: The trailers-only diagnostic MUST receive HTTP status 200, no response DATA message, grpc-status INVALID_ARGUMENT, and the exact bounded grpc-message from the canonical service contract.

requirements.title: Deadline and cancellation semantics
requirements.statement: Deadline-exceeded and client-cancellation diagnostics MUST remain distinct, MUST disable retry and hedging, MUST identify the initiating lifecycle event, and MUST require the expected terminal status without treating it as an unexpected operation failure.

requirements.title: Compression and metadata semantics
requirements.statement: The gzip diagnostic MUST validate decompressed protobuf bytes and record encoder provenance without requiring byte-identical gzip output; the fixed-metadata diagnostic MUST use the exact ASCII and binary metadata profile declared by the canonical service contract.

requirements.title: Message-size boundaries
requirements.statement: Empty-message and one-mebibyte-message diagnostics MUST declare payload, protobuf, and gRPC envelope lengths and hashes, including the proto3 omission of an empty bytes field.

requirements.title: Channel lifecycle isolation
requirements.statement: Pre-established-channel and new-channel-per-operation scenarios MUST use separate scenario identities and measured windows; channel establishment time MUST NOT be mixed into reused-channel RPC latency.
